title: OrganiGram Holdings (OGI) Q3 2020 Earnings Call Transcript
text: OrganiGram Holdings Q3 2020 Earnings Call Summary. Financial Performance: Revenue & Margins: Q3 net revenue: $18 million (down from $24.8 million in Q3 2019). Q3 adult-use recreational revenue: $15.3 million (slightly up from Q2's $15 million). Gross margin before fair value changes: negative $26.4 million (vs. positive $12.3 million prior year). Key Charges: The company took approximately $29 million in non-normal charges: $19.3 million in excess of unsalable inventory and $7.9 million of charges related to a reduced workforce due to COVID-19. Operational Metrics - Workforce & Production: Temporarily laid off 45% of workforce in early April. Permanently reduced workforce by approximately 25%. Paused pre-roll production (which comprised 14% of Q2 revenue). Deferred Phase 4C completion indefinitely, recognizing $37.7 million impairment charge. Cash Position: Generated positive operating cash flow of $8.5 million in Q3. Ended quarter with $44.8 million in cash and short-term investments. Post-quarter: approximately $78 million in cash and investments. Company Outlook: Management signaled that meaningful incremental revenue gains would likely not occur until Q1 fiscal 2021, emphasizing that recent product launches needed time to gain market traction. The company expects continued gross margin pressure in Q4 from production inefficiencies and unabsorbed overhead costs.
